process_name: Flavor-changing neutral-current top decay t -> u g
implementation_difficulty_reason: Limit cataloging is MEDIUM, but production-grade integration is HIGH because the repo lacks a top chromomagnetic FCNC operator convention, RS-to-SMEFT matching, t -> u g width and u g -> t production normalization, collider/PDF reinterpretation machinery, and QCD-running choices for this operator.

evidence: Required plan greps rerun on 2026-05-16; no source-code implementation of t -> u g, tug, tqg, C_uG, or u g -> t found.
Targeted implementation grep found only generic FCNC prose/comment hits at quarkConstraints/PAPER_0710_1869.md:35 and tests/test_paper_couplings.py:258, not an observable implementation.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 enumerates only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 in the modern quark policy surface.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:167 says the modern surface is policy-only and does not define a numeric EFT backend.
grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints/ qcd/ flavorConstraints/ neutrinos/ yukawa/ warpConfig/ solvers/ scanParams/ tests/ -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints/ qcd/ flavorConstraints/ neutrinos/ yukawa/ warpConfig/ solvers/ scanParams/ tests/ -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints/ qcd/ flavorConstraints/ neutrinos/ yukawa/ warpConfig/ solvers/ scanParams/ tests/ -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "t.?->.?u.?g|t.?->.?g.?u|tug|utg|u.?g.?->.?t|tqg|uG|gtu|FCNC|flavou?r-changing|flavor changing" quarkConstraints/ qcd/ flavorConstraints/ neutrinos/ yukawa/ warpConfig/ solvers/ scanParams/ tests/ -g "!*.ipynb"

issues: CHK-1: TeX cites the SM B(t -> u g) approximately 3.6e-14 context value, but that numerical claim is stored in theory_context rather than pdg_or_equivalent.values with the strict CA metadata placement required for all TeX numerical claims.

status_history.reason: Promoted the TeX-cited SM B(t -> u g) context value into pdg_or_equivalent.values with complete source metadata per CA CHK-1 finding.
Cycle-3 checker re-check passed CHK-1 through CHK-8 after WA-v2.
Opus round 2 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_002_index.md.

status_history.notes: Fetched cited PDG/arXiv/collaboration sources; measured value claims match.

status_history.note: B: down-sector alignment promotes up-sector observables

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(t\to u g)\).
This entry covers the flavor-changing neutral-current top decay \(t\to u g\),
catalog id T006 in the top/Higgs/electroweak family.  Experimentally it is
constrained mainly through anomalous single-top production, \(u g\to t\), then
translated into an equivalent top-decay branching fraction under a specified
chromomagnetic-operator convention.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical experimental source is the PDG/pdgLive Q007TUG top-FCNC gluon
datablock, accessed on 2026-05-16
(\texttt{PDG2025:T006:ATLAS2022:t\_ug};
\texttt{references/T006/pdg\_2025\_top\_tug.txt}).  Its current ATLAS entry,
AAD 2022T, uses \(139~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) of \(13~\mathrm{TeV}\) \(pp\) data
(\texttt{PDG2025:T006:ATLAS2022:dataset}).  For the up-gluon production mode
it gives
\[
  \sigma(ug\to t)\,\mathcal{B}(t\to bW)\,
  \mathcal{B}(W\to \ell\nu)<3.0~\mathrm{pb}
\]
at 95\% CL (\texttt{PDG2025:T006:ATLAS2022:ug\_to\_t\_cross\_section}), with
the ATLAS leptonic-\(W\) sum \(\mathcal{B}(W\to\ell\nu)=0.325\)
(\texttt{PDG2025:T006:ATLAS2022:w\_leptonic\_sum}).  The same entry gives
\[
  |C^{ut}_{uG}|/\Lambda^2 < 0.057~\mathrm{TeV}^{-2},
  \qquad
  \mathcal{B}(t\to u g)<6.1\times10^{-5}.
\]
These coefficient and branching-fraction limits are recorded as
\texttt{PDG2025:T006:ATLAS2022:CuGut} and
\texttt{PDG2025:T006:ATLAS2022:t\_ug}.
The PDG block also records the CMS 7 and 8 TeV comparison result based on
\(5.0~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) and \(19.7~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\)
(\texttt{CMS2017:T006:dataset}):
\(|\kappa_{tug}|/\Lambda<4.1\times10^{-3}~\mathrm{TeV}^{-1}\), corresponding
to \(\mathcal{B}(t\to u g)<2.0\times10^{-5}\)
(\texttt{CMS2017:T006:kappa\_tug}; \texttt{CMS2017:T006:t\_ug}).  For SM
context, not as a measured PDG value, Aguilar-Saavedra's charm-mode result and
CKM scaling imply
\(\mathcal{B}(t\to u g)\simeq3.6\times10^{-14}\)
(\texttt{AguilarSaavedra2004:T006:t\_ug\_SM}).
sections.relevance:
This channel probes color-carrying first--third generation up-sector flavor
violation rather than the neutral-meson \(\Delta F=2\) four-quark operators
already audited in the quark scan.  In anarchic warped flavor, the heavy color
sector and the IR-localized top can generate non-universal mass-basis couplings
and loop-level chromomagnetic dipoles.  A \(t\to u g\) limit is therefore a
direct top-flavor diagnostic, complementary to \(D^0\) mixing and to
electroweak channels such as \(t\to uZ\) or \(t\to uH\).
sections.post_2008:
The paper-era CFW reference point, arXiv:0804.1954, predates the LHC
single-top FCNC program and is used here only as RS-flavor context.  Since
then, CMS and ATLAS have interpreted single-top data in top-gluon FCNC
operators, with the ATLAS Run-2 dataset supplying the current PDG/pdgLive
entry.  The resulting bounds remain far above the SM prediction, so they are
new-physics limits rather than SM precision tests.
sections.validity:
The experimental upper bound is clean because the SM signal is negligible at
collider sensitivity.  Its use as an RS scan cut is model dependent: ATLAS
quotes a left-handed SMEFT \(C_{uG}^{ut}\) interpretation, while CMS uses an
older \(\kappa_{tug}\) convention.  The conversion from single-top production
to \({\cal B}(t\to u g)\) assumes one FCNC operator at a time, standard top
decays, and a specific normalization of the chromomagnetic interaction.  A
model producing both chiralities, contact terms, or correlated heavy-gluon
resonance effects needs a dedicated reinterpretation.

In down-aligned or kaon-protected RS variants, top FCNCs become leading rather
than secondary diagnostics.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The required plan greps were rerun on 2026-05-16, followed by a
targeted search for \(t\to u g\), \(tug\), \(utg\), \(tqg\), \(C_{uG}\), and
\(ug\to t\) across \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/}.  The only implementation-directory hits were generic FCNC
prose/comments at \texttt{quarkConstraints/PAPER\_0710\_1869.md:35} and
\texttt{tests/test\_paper\_couplings.py:258}.  The modern phenomenology surface
enumerates only \(\epsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D^0\) at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}, and states that it is
policy-only at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:167}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Cataloging the external limit is straightforward, but live
integration would need a top chromomagnetic FCNC operator convention,
RS-to-SMEFT matching, decay-width and \(ug\to t\) production normalization,
PDF/collider acceptance assumptions or a validated recast, and QCD-running
choices not present in the existing \(\Delta F=2\) code.

priority_rationale_short: tug single-top bound is direct but subleading
priority_rationale_full: The t -> u g constraint directly probes first-third generation chromomagnetic flavor violation and benefits from the up-quark parton luminosity in single-top searches. It still depends on a specific anomalous-coupling normalization and one-operator-at-a-time reinterpretation. In an anarchic RS scan it is a diagnostic top-FCNC check rather than a leading mass-scale setter.
